name: Numaligarh Refinery Limited
oneLine: 45+ subdomains visible in CT including VPN, AD, document mgmt; no breach detected

tls.note: cert chain valid; testssl HPKP SPKI mismatch — minor

emailAuth.note: Tighten to p=reject after 30-day report monitoring; verify mailersend.net include is current

lookalikes.note: Australian National Rugby League — distinct org

findings: 45+ subdomains visible in public CT logs — full infrastructure topology mappable from outside
VPN gateway (vpn.nrl.co.in) and AD-related service (adss.nrl.co.in) named publicly
Document management exposed across dev/uat/prod tiers (ieddms.*)
Self-hosted RAG/LLM tool visible (open-web-ui.nrlrag) — introduces AI-specific threat surface
Public tender PDFs include Enterprise Risk Management Framework (governance content alongside tender attachments)
No breach evidence in HIBP, IntelX, GitHub code search, paste archives, or S3/Azure bucket guessing
Email auth strong (DMARC p=quarantine, strict SPF)

eolFlags: VPN appliance brand to be confirmed by CISO

attackPaths.name: VPN appliance pre-auth RCE
attackPaths.entry: Pre-auth RCE on VPN appliance (CVE depends on brand: F5 CVE-2025-53521, Citrix CVE-2025-7775/5777, Fortinet CVE-2023-27997)
attackPaths.pivot: Session-token theft, MFA bypass via memory leak (CitrixBleed-class)
attackPaths.objective: Authenticated VPN session as legitimate-looking user; internal network access

attackPaths.name: hmis triple-EOL exploitation
attackPaths.entry: Any unpatched PHP 7.4 / OpenSSL 1.0.2 / Apache 2.4.6 CVE since 2022
attackPaths.pivot: Web shell deployment, lateral movement via stolen credentials
attackPaths.objective: Foothold in NRL corporate estate via the HMIS host

attackPaths.name: AI surface prompt-injection / vector-store exfil
attackPaths.entry: Malicious document submitted to RAG knowledge base, or prompt injection via user channel
attackPaths.pivot: Extract API keys via LLM-mediated channel; manipulate model output trusted by automation
attackPaths.objective: Data exfiltration or trust manipulation in NRL's AI tooling

attackPaths.name: Policy-document lure to executive mailbox
attackPaths.objective: Corporate VPN access via legitimate user session

attackPaths.name: IIS/ASP.NET fingerprint to RCE
attackPaths.entry: HTTP.sys or IIS-specific CVE on inetdemo or websvc (CVE-2023-36434)
attackPaths.pivot: SYSTEM-level access on Windows IIS host

cisoPatchList.title: hmis.nrl.co.in triple-EOL stack migration
cisoPatchList.fix: Migrate to RHEL 9 / Ubuntu 24.04 LTS (Apache 2.4.62+, PHP 8.x, OpenSSL 3.x). Interim: IP-allowlist via firewall.
cisoPatchList.validation: curl -sI https://hmis.nrl.co.in | grep -iE 'server|x-powered-by' shows Apache 2.4.62+ and PHP 8.x

cisoPatchList.title: nrlportal.nrl.co.in stack upgrade during 503 window
cisoPatchList.fix: Same as hmis. Currently in 503 maintenance — opportune upgrade window.
cisoPatchList.validation: curl -sI https://nrlportal.nrl.co.in returns HTTP/2 200 with Apache 2.4.62+ and OpenSSL 3.x

cisoPatchList.title: VPN appliance brand confirmation + KEV patch
cisoPatchList.fix: CISO confirms appliance brand internally. Apply current vendor patch for that brand. Enforce session-token rotation post-patch.
cisoPatchList.validation: Vendor command (e.g. 'get system status' on FortiGate) shows firmware at or above patched version

cisoPatchList.title: IIS 10.0 monthly SU + version-leak suppression
cisoPatchList.fix: Apply current Windows monthly cumulative update. Strip Server and X-AspNet-Version headers via URL Rewrite.
cisoPatchList.validation: curl -sI shows headers absent or generic

cisoPatchList.title: ASP.NET MyServer1 cluster version audit + header strip
cisoPatchList.fix: CISO confirms ASP.NET runtime version internally. Set httpRuntime enableVersionHeader=false in Web.config.

cisoPatchList.title: Apache Tomcat 9.0.70 → 9.0.99+
cisoPatchList.fix: Standard Tomcat upgrade: download 9.0.99, swap bin/lib, retain conf/webapps, restart.
cisoPatchList.validation: curl -sI https://shapp.nrl.co.in | grep -i server shows Tomcat 9.0.99+

cisoPatchList.title: nginx 1.20.1 → 1.26.x or 1.28.x stable
cisoPatchList.validation: curl -sI https://stps.nrl.co.in | grep -i server shows nginx 1.26+

cisoPatchList.title: procurixai AI surface threat-model audit
cisoPatchList.fix: WAF + rate-limiting + API key audit + SSO with conditional access + full request/response logging.
cisoPatchList.validation: WAF rules in place; audit log of last 30 days; SSO confirmed

cisoPatchList.title: Publish /.well-known/security.txt
cisoPatchList.fix: Static file at /.well-known/security.txt with Contact/Expires/Encryption/Acknowledgments per RFC 9116.

cisoPatchList.title: Forward-only certificate-transparency policy
cisoPatchList.fix: New internal-only services use private CA (AD CS / Vault PKI). External services use wildcard certs at apex.
cisoPatchList.validation: New internal service certificates after policy adoption do not appear in crt.sh queries

cisoPatchList.title: DMARC tighten to p=reject
cisoPatchList.fix: 30-day report monitoring; verify mailersend.net include; update DMARC TXT to p=reject.

cisoPatchList.title: Internal-IP cluster network audit
cisoPatchList.fix: For internal-only services: remove public DNS records (split-horizon DNS). For internet-exposed but firewall-blocked: document the rule.
cisoPatchList.validation: Internal subdomains no longer appear in external DNS

cisoPatchList.title: RAG governance audit (open-web-ui.nrlrag)
cisoPatchList.fix: Document RAG sources, audit API key handling, enforce SSO, conversation logging to SOC, output-trust validation.
cisoPatchList.validation: Governance review document on file; SSO enforced; logs in SOC tooling

phase2.headlineQuestion: What is the firmware version of the NRL VPN appliance, and is it patched against the KEV-listed pre-authentication RCE for that brand?
